commit.message:
Align portfolio with resume ATS updates

- Add B2B to enterprise team descriptions (Home, About)
- Add 'problem definition through launch' to About and Contact CTAs
- Add 'success metrics' to Enterprise Analytics role card
- Update HOW section: 'prototype in code, then refine in canvas'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: audit — test coverage + complexity elimination

Phase 1: Test coverage (+32 tests)
- validation.rs: 15 tests for all 6 public functions (was 0)
- routing.rs: 8 edge case tests (non-numeric, array Contains, type mismatch)
- orchestrator.rs: 5 tests (has_session, cleanup_stale, check_bounds paths)
- interrupts.rs: 4 error path tests (invalid resolve/cancel, multi-interrupt)

Phase 2: Complexity elimination
- Consolidate bounds checking: Envelope::check_bounds() as single source of truth,
  at_limit() becomes thin wrapper — deleted redundant free function
- Remove terminated state duplication: PipelineSession.terminated removed,
  envelope.bounds.terminated is sole authority (5 write sites unified)
- Extract Kernel::process_agent_result(): eliminates fragile PCB reconciliation
  arithmetic (saturating_sub/max(0) → direct delta application)

Post-audit improvements:
- Test fixtures: empty_ctx()/outputs_with() in routing, test_response() in interrupts
- #[derive(Default)] on AgentExecutionMetrics (compiler-offloaded construction)
- Eliminated metrics.clone() via scalar extraction before ownership transfer

276 unit tests + 17 integration tests pass, cargo clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Replace React default import with type-only ReactNode in Features.tsx

Switches from `import React from 'react'` (used only for `React.ReactNode`)
to `import type { ReactNode } from 'react'` — erased at compile time, zero
runtime cost, consistent with the jsx-react-jsx transform and the pattern
used throughout the rest of the codebase.

commit.message:
[consensus] Remove randomness fast path code (#18870)

* [consensus] Remove randomness fast path

The randomness fast path used a separate, higher secrecy threshold
(via ConfigV2) for faster randomness reconstruction. It was disabled
by default and the mainnet on-chain config was reverted from V2 to V1.
This removes all fast path runtime code while preserving backward
compatibility with BCS-serialized on-chain and local DB state.

Key decisions:
- Transcripts.fast and AugmentedData.fast_delta Option fields are
  retained (always None) for BCS deserialization compatibility
- Move ConfigV2 struct is kept (published on-chain type) but new_v2()
  is removed
- Key pair deserialization handles old format gracefully by falling
  through to regenerate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix formatting and rebuild cached packages

Apply cargo +nightly fmt and rebuild aptos-cached-packages after
removing new_v2() from randomness_config.move.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Return error on key pair deserialization failure instead of regenerating

Regenerating a new key pair on deserialization failure could cause a
mismatch with stale CertifiedAugData already persisted in storage,
leading to incorrect randomness shares. Instead, return an error to
disable randomness for the epoch, which is the safer behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Keep old key pair format for DB backward compatibility

Instead of migrating to a new serialization format, keep the old
(AugKeyPair, Option<AugKeyPair>) format and always write None for
the fast path key pair. This avoids any migration complexity and
ensures existing DB entries remain readable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Rebuild cached packages after rebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: keep rounding and Move code unchanged for safety

Per review feedback:
- Restore rounding code to original (pass None for fast path threshold
  instead of removing the parameter) to ensure replay safety since
  rounding is called inside VM during DKG transaction verification.
- Restore new_v2() in randomness_config.move since it's a published
  public function on the framework.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Rebuild cached packages after rebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Keep DKGPvssConfig.fast_wconfig for BCS backward compatibility

Retain the fast_wconfig: Option<SSConfig> field in DKGPvssConfig
(always set to None) to preserve BCS serialization compatibility,
consistent with the approach taken for Transcripts.fast and
AugmentedData.fast_delta.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: handle on-chain ConfigV2 gracefully

Fix tests from recent rebase that still referenced removed FastShare
type and old 4-argument verify() signature. Remove the fast share
sender verification test (no longer applicable) and update remaining
tests to use the new 3-argument verify().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting in DKGPvssConfig PartialEq

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Address review: handle on-chain ConfigV2 gracefully

Keep ConfigV2 in OnChainRandomnessConfig enum for replay safety.
Historical DKG transactions may have used V2, and removing it from
TryFrom causes randomness_config_derived() to return None, which
would produce different DKGRounding parameters and break transcript
verification during blockchain replay.

V2 is now treated identically to V1: its secrecy_threshold and
reconstruction_threshold are used, but fast_path_secrecy_threshold
is ignored (fast path has been removed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Retain fast path secrecy threshold for rounding replay safety

Thread the fast_path_secrecy_threshold from on-chain ConfigV2 through
to DKGRounding::new, so that replay of historical V2-era DKG
transactions produces identical rounding parameters. For V1/Off
configs, None is passed (no fast path), preserving current behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Pass fast_wconfig from DKGRounding to DKGPvssConfig

Thread the fast_wconfig produced by DKGRounding through to
DKGPvssConfig instead of always using None. This ensures that
during replay of V2-era epochs, the DKGPvssConfig is constructed
with the same fast_wconfig as the original, matching the on-chain
BCS-serialized config exactly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Restore release builder V2 config and new_v2 constructor

Revert the removal of V2 variant from ReleaseFriendlyRandomnessConfig
and restore OnChainRandomnessConfig::new_v2() constructor. These are
needed for backward compatibility — removing them could break framework
upgrade compatibility checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Fix nightly formatting in build_dkg_pvss_config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Remove unused lru dependency

The lru crate was only used for the fast path broadcast cache
(blocks_with_broadcasted_fast_shares) which has been removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [consensus] Update Cargo.lock for lru removal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Phase 6 — render pipeline, captions, aspect ratio + face tracking

Renderer:
- ffmpeg_builder: filter_complex from TimelineModel (concat, xfade, title cards)
- preview_renderer: libx264 ultrafast, 1280px for fast scrubbing
- final_renderer: NVENC with auto-fallback to libx264 slow
- queue_manager: RenderJob state machine + ffprobe duration

Caption engine:
- generator: timeline-relative captions from TranscriptionSegments
- styler: ASS header + events, word_highlight karaoke tags (\\kf)
- platform presets: tiktok (Impact/72pt), youtube, instagram
- hex_to_ass_color: correct &HAABBGGRR channel reversal
- burner: standalone ASS burn-in for re-render after edit

Aspect ratio:
- detector: MediaPipe face detection with graceful import fallback
- reframer: center-crop or face-centered crop → ffmpeg_vf string
- tracker: temporal smoothing (rolling avg) for stable reframing

Routers: /api/render/* (6 endpoints), /api/captions/* (8 endpoints)
RenderJob.render_params column added (auto-migrated on startup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: show all bulk-added properties in onboarding wizard (#182)

The bulk add loop called setHouses([...houses, newHouse]) where houses
was captured once at the start of the loop (stale closure). Each
iteration overwrote the previous, so only the last property appeared.
Switched to functional updater setHouses(prev => [...prev, newHouse])
so each iteration correctly appends to the latest state.

Closes #145

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix infinite loop in delete branch modal

Remove `deleteModalBranch` from useEffect dependencies to prevent
infinite re-renders. The derived object reference changed on every
parent re-render, causing the merge check API call to loop endlessly.
Now the branch lookup happens inside the effect instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update skill for Astro 6+: add PRG pattern to Layer 1

Astro 6 stabilizes the Session API, enabling the PRG (Post-Redirect-Get)
pattern for Layer 1 form processing. This eliminates the browser's
"resubmit form?" warning on reload — strengthening Layer 1's "never
breaks" guarantee at the HTTP level.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: An architecture for building healthy web applications by trusting the browser. Design from failure modes.

commit.message:
fix: use 100svh instead of padding for iOS Safari toolbar fix

The previous approach of padding-bottom on #root didn't work because
height: 100% resolves to the full viewport on iOS Safari (behind toolbar).
100svh (small viewport height) gives the actual visible height with the
toolbar showing. Supported since Safari 15.4 (March 2022).
Padding fallback kept for rare older browsers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: event-centric map rebuild — NukeMap replaces UnifiedMap

Replace 2,700-line monolith with modular 2,176-line event-centric map.
Server-driven data from vehicle_location_observations (265K observations)
instead of client-side 195K vehicle array with fake geocoding.

- DB: populated VLO from vehicle_images GPS + vehicles table coordinates
- Edge function: map-vehicles queries VLO with confidence/time/event filters + histogram mode
- Frontend: NukeMap.tsx + hooks/layers/controls/panels architecture
- Deleted: UnifiedMap.tsx, mapUtils.ts (geo() fake locations), us-zips.json (824KB)
- New: EventPinDetail, MapTimeline, confidence tiers, temporal filtering
- ZCTA boundaries uploaded to Supabase Storage (map-data bucket)

Fix SW-P-08 crosspoint query readback

The Ultrix sends ACK then tally data as separate messages. Previously

Add pace/speed input modes for Running and Cycling exercises

- Cycling uses speed in mph (e.g., 15 mph)

feat: Add ci npm script, typecheck alias, and fix Prettier formatting

- Add `typecheck` script as alias for `tsc --noEmit`
- Add `ci` script that runs all checks in sequence: format:check, lint,
  typecheck, test, build — mirrors what a CI workflow would execute

commit.message:
fix(https): address Copilot review issues for HTTPS mode (#98)

- Fix PEM/DER mismatch: generate ca.der via openssl x509 -outform der
  so caCertDerBytes returns true DER bytes for /ca.crt and mobileconfig
- Add backwards-compat: regenerate ca.der if missing from existing installs
- Add openssl availability check before TlsManager.init() in both
  startServerCli() and startServer(); throws clear error if not found
- Add CA certificate SHA-256 fingerprint to setup page and server log
  so users can verify the cert before installing (MITM mitigation)
- Remove unused basic_utils dependency (openssl-based impl is used instead)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: auto-detect user ID column type for UUID/int compatibility

The user_id migration column now inspects the users table primary key
type at migration time, using uuid() for string-based IDs and
unsignedBigInteger() for numeric IDs. Also fixes job timeout default
and README queue worker docs.

commit.message:
fix: remove turbo.json to stop Vercel Turbo detection

turbo.json/package.json/tsconfig.json at root are leftover monorepo
boilerplate. Vercel detects turbo.json and overrides all vercel.json
settings, causing 'cd frontend: No such file or directory'. Removing
them lets vercel.json with rootDirectory:frontend take effect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add debug logging for startCommit flow

Log when:
- headCommit is captured during sandbox creation (server)
- startCommit is sent in done event (server)
- startCommit is received by client (branch-list)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: landing page v5 — new design, K2C section, IRR CTA wiring

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Pass startCommit from sandbox creation to client

The startCommit was being saved to the database but not sent back
to the client in the SSE response. This meant newly created branches
had startCommit=null in the client state until a page refresh.

Now we include startCommit in the "done" event and update the branch
state with it on the client side.

Set macro-focused dashboard default for landing page users and fix mobile UI

Landing Page Default:
- Users from /protein-tracker or /macro-tracker get dashboard focus set to 'macros'
- Automatically shows macro progress bars at top of dashboard
- Added saveDashboardFocus import

Mobile UI Fixes (CommonFoods.jsx):
- Responsive gaps: gap-2 on mobile, gap-4 on desktop
- Smaller Add button text on mobile (text-sm sm:text-base)
- Reduced button padding on mobile (px-3 sm:px-4)
- Added flex-shrink-0 to button container to prevent squishing
- Added min-w-0 to content area for proper text truncation
- Gap between buttons reduced to gap-1 on mobile
- Flex-wrap on food name/category to prevent overflow

These changes prevent buttons from extending outside margins on mobile.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Fix all clippy warnings for CI

- Collapse consecutive str::replace calls into array pattern
- Use div_ceil() instead of manual (a + b - 1) / b
- Replace useless format!("literal") with "literal".to_string()
- Use vec![...] initializer instead of Vec::new() + push
- Use enumerate() instead of indexing with loop variable
- Inline string literal in eprintln format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: Record probe baseline for code-fix task f1aae575

Root cause of recurring CI workflow push failure:
The GitHub PAT for this product has only 'repo' scope.
GitHub requires 'workflow' scope to create/update .github/workflows/ files.

Fix required: Platform admin must regenerate the PAT with 'repo' + 'workflow'
scopes and update it in the product settings (Product ID: d730a230-b571-50b0-b074-9ac09c11bf02).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

in bash -c scripts from corrupting the terminal display.

commit.message:
[docs](trx-rs): add virtual channels design document

Describes the per-rig dynamic virtual channel architecture for SDR rigs:
session binding via SSE session_id, channel lifecycle (ref-counted,
auto-freed on last subscriber disconnect), center-freq conflict rules,
per-channel audio WebSocket and decode SSE, frontend picker UI, and
phased implementation plan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Stan Grams <sjg@haxx.space>

repository.name: trx-rs
repository.description: Experimental ham rig controller written in Rust - work in progress

commit.message:
Fix landing page navigation issues properly

Removed problematic redirect loop and fixed state initialization:

1. Detect ?from=landing-page query parameter BEFORE state initialization
2. Set landingPageShown and installPromptShown initial state based on parameter
3. Mark storage flags immediately when parameter detected
4. Clean up URL with replaceState to prevent re-detection

This fixes:
- React landing page now correctly skipped for users from Astro pages
- No infinite reload loop for existing users on Astro URLs
- State initialized correctly before render

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Amazon link to The Old Fisherman book

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add community guidelines gating for reviews and fix wizard already-reviewed UX

Three improvements to the review system:

1. Replace the report icon on review cards with a proper flag SVG matching the
   comment system for visual consistency.

2. Gate review submissions behind the existing community guidelines agreement
   flow (shared Profile.guidelines_agreed field). Covers all review entry points:
   review hub detail page, review hub replies, and Rate My Games wizard.
   Extracts guidelines modals into shared partials for DRY reuse across
   comments, review hub, and wizard templates.

3. Fix the Rate My Games wizard for already-reviewed games: instead of showing
   an empty interactive form that silently blocks submission, display the user's
   existing review with recommend badge and body text. Adds an "Edit Review"
   flow that pre-fills the form and uses PUT to update, with a cancel button to
   return to the display view.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
